title: mkcert-trusted FAPI cert (the browser-login render fix). A fresh browser at a demo's next-web renders the signed-in app with ZERO manual cert-trust, by minting a locally-trusted (mkcert) FAPI TLS cert at bring-up, degrading cleanly to the openssl self-signed path when mkcert is absent / DEMO_NO_MKCERT=1 / a mint fails. ONE branch in up-injected.sh step 3a-bis (inside the keep-existing-cert guard), the openssl fallback factored into gen_openssl_fapi_cert(), non-fatal throughout. ZERO change to the cert-CONSUMING side (fake-fapi/main.go, gen_injected_override.py mount, inject.py) — all path-only.
position_note: M31 is the 1st of 2 milestones of v1.7 'house lights' (M31 -> M32, strictly sequential — both touch up-injected.sh + the same demo doc cluster). M32 = studio-desk single-port/production + the :9100 sweep. After this close: M32 is the active milestone; v1.7 is NOT yet ready for close-release.
kind: a two-repo milestone. The CODE is in the ext authoring copy (rosetta-extensions @ tag house-lights-m31 @ 6565ef8; harden @ 815993f; close review-fix @ 5022e72): the mkcert branch + gen_openssl_fapi_cert fallback + DEMO_NO_MKCERT parse + the FapiCertStep test class (11 tests) + the gen_injected_override.py comment touch-up + the close README count reconciliation. The rosetta side carries the recipe-browser-login.md §B rewrite + frontend-tier.md one-liner + the demo-up SKILL note + the planning artifacts. close-milestone's Phase 11 merge is rosetta-only (m31/mkcert-fapi-cert -> release/01.70-house-lights); the ORCHESTRATOR finalizes the ext side (ff main + re-point tag + delete branch).

sections.list: mkcert branch (idempotent -install + mint 127.0.0.1 localhost ::1) inside keep-existing guard · openssl fallback kept verbatim (absent AND mint-fail), non-fatal · DEMO_NO_MKCERT=1 opt-out · code comments retired + dev-N shared-helper forward-note · recipe-browser-login.md §B manual->automatic (+ security/remote/Firefox/expiry notes) · frontend-tier.md cert one-liner · demo-up SKILL note (+ DEMO_NO_MKCERT in argument-hint) · ZERO change to the 3 cert-consumers verified (M31-D4) · README-index guard exit 0 · ext tag house-lights-m31 · close-time browser-trust verify by composition (M31-D7)

field_bugs_surfaced.note: M31 is a tooling-change milestone (not a field-bake). The harden pass surfaced ONE test-harness fidelity issue (M31-D6: the extracted-block harnesses sourced under `set -uo pipefail` but production runs `set -euo pipefail`, so the install-failure test would false-green) — a test-harness fix, not a production bug; up-injected.sh was byte-identical before and after. No production code defects found at build, harden, or close.

coverage.note: /harden-milestone ran (1 pass, 2026-06-15). The milestone surface is one bash branch (no line-coverage tool applies to bash); 'coverage' = branch enumeration. The 8 build FapiCertStep tests covered the 4 main branches (mkcert-ok / mint-fail / DEMO_NO_MKCERT / mkcert-absent) + the keep-existing idempotency guard; the +3 harden tests closed the genuine remaining edges (install-failure-via-||true mutation-proven, $CERTS-whitespace-quoting across both branches, crt-only partial-state guard). Values-blind throughout (tests read only fapi.crt's first line to discriminate which branch fired; never assert on fapi.key body).

benchmarks.note: No performance-sensitive code in M31 (a cert-mint branch at bring-up).

review.findings_note: 2 findings: 0 scope-gap (every In-list item delivered Fate-1) · 0 code-quality (the ext branch is well-factored + tested green) · 1 docs (README.md count drift) · 0 tests · 0 decision-triage-blend (the cert behaviors were already blended at build/harden) · 1 adversarial-scenario record. Both landed Fate-1.
review.scope: GREEN — all 11 deliverable boxes checked. Every overview In-list item delivered. The close-time live-render verify was satisfied by composition (M31-D7: chromium default-context trusts the mkcert cert / 200 vs rejects the openssl self-signed / ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID + the earlier cert-trusted->renders proof + the 11 FapiCertStep tests). No silent drops; zero code TODO/FIXME/HACK in any M31-touched file.
review.code_quality: GREEN — 0 findings. The ext branch reviewed cross-cuttingly: gen_openssl_fapi_cert() factors the openssl fallback into ONE definition called from both fallback branches (no two-copy drift risk); if-then-else (no SC2015 hazard); PATH-based `command -v mkcert` detection (not a hard-coded /opt/homebrew path, M31-D3); non-fatal throughout (the never-abort-a-good-bring-up contract); the outer keep-existing guard intact (idempotent re-ups). shellcheck 0.11.0 clean; py_compile clean.
review.adversarial: GREEN — 1 scenario (Phase 2c) recorded in decisions.md: a successful-exit-code cert mint that writes a zero-byte/truncated fapi.crt -> the existence-only keep-guard ([ ! -f ]) would keep the corrupt cert on re-up. Verdict: NOT a regression (identical to the pre-M31 openssl-only existence guard; M31 changed which tool mints, not the guard) + already-documented repair path (recipe §B 'Cert expiry': rm fapi.crt + re-up). No code change; the existence-guard semantics are pinned by test_func_keep_existing_cert_is_idempotent + test_func_keep_existing_guard_keys_on_crt_only_partial_state.
review.docs: GREEN after Phase 7 — 1 must-fix reconciled: demo-stack/README.md quoted '13 unit tests' (x2, stale since ~M16/M28; actual 50) -> reconciled to 50 + a one-line note distinguishing it from GUIDE's guarded '28' curated-subset (M31-D8). The 4 rosetta doc diffs (recipe-browser-login.md §B, frontend-tier.md, demo-up SKILL) match the shipped shell branch; cross-refs resolve; README-index guard exit 0.
review.tests: GREEN — test_tooling.py 50/50 + test_frontend_build.py 42/42; flake gate 5/5 randomized sequential. No new test gaps (FapiCertStep covers all 4 branches + idempotency + install-failure + whitespace + partial-state). The README count fix touched no test.
review.decision_triage: M31-D1..D8 reviewed. The cert-step user/developer-facing BEHAVIORS (4 branches, ||true swallow, SANs, fallback, security/remote/Firefox/expiry caveats) were already blended into recipe-browser-login.md §B + frontend-tier.md + the demo-up SKILL during build/harden — verified current, no re-blend needed. D1/D2/D3/D6 (factoring, test-harness mechanics, strict-mode lesson) maintainer-only -> archive in decisions.md. D4 (no-touch consumers) already in recipe §B + the code comment. D5 (forward-note) lives at the code site. D7 (compose verify) + D8 (count reconciliation) maintainer-only -> archive.

notes: M31 is the 1st of 2 milestones of v1.7 'house lights' — the demo-UI-hardening release triggered by a live next-web blank page (clerk-js's handshake to the fake FAPI hit an untrusted self-signed cert). M31 automates a locally-trusted mkcert FAPI cert into the demo bring-up: ONE branch in up-injected.sh step 3a-bis (inside the keep-existing-cert guard), the openssl fallback factored into gen_openssl_fapi_cert() and kept byte-compatible, DEMO_NO_MKCERT=1 opt-out, non-fatal throughout, with ZERO change to the 3 cert-consuming files (path-only). The close-time observable verify was proven by composition (M31-D7: chromium trusts the mkcert cert / 200 vs rejects the openssl self-signed / ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID — the exact blank-page cause — + the earlier cert-trusted->renders proof + the 11 FapiCertStep functional/edge tests; demo-3 had been torn down so an end-to-end demo render wasn't re-spun, which is necessary+sufficient by composition). The close review found 2 findings, both Fate-1: the demo-stack/README.md '13 tests' count drift (reconciled to 50) + a recorded adversarial scenario (zero-byte cert / existence-only guard = pre-existing, documented repair path). Go 1027 unchanged (M31 touched no Go); Python +11 (the FapiCertStep class); flake 0; deferral audit GREEN. After this close: M32 studio-desk single-port/production is the active milestone.
